questions.question: You are given a free afternoon to work on something interesting. What sounds most satisfying?

options.text: Making something look unique, fun, or more appealing
Organizing how everything should fit together before starting
Figuring out how a machine, device, or setup works behind the scenes
Looking for patterns, rules, or clues to understand what is really going on

questions.question: When something in daily life goes wrong, what do you usually do first?

options.text: Try different fixes until you find what works
Step back and think about the whole situation before acting
Ask people questions and gather different opinions
Notice tiny details that others may have missed

questions.question: Which kind of school or personal project would you enjoy most?

options.text: Designing the style, message, or experience people will notice first
Planning the steps so everything runs smoothly from start to finish
Collecting information and discovering useful insights from it
Building, connecting, or adjusting real objects or equipment

questions.question: In a group activity, which role feels most natural to you?

options.text: The person who keeps everyone connected and understood
The person who makes the plan and keeps things organized
The person who checks for mistakes and improves weak spots
The person who brings fresh ideas and a different style

questions.question: Imagine you are setting up a new room. What matters most to you?

options.text: That it feels welcoming and easy for people to use
That everything has a clear place and purpose
That devices, connections, or moving parts work reliably
That nothing important is left exposed or easy to misuse

questions.question: Which type of puzzle sounds most fun?

options.text: A logic puzzle with clues and hidden patterns
A puzzle where you must arrange pieces into the best structure
A puzzle where you improve something until it works better
A puzzle where you imagine new possibilities and creative solutions

questions.question: When you learn something new, what usually helps you most?

options.text: Seeing a clear step-by-step path
Understanding the big picture first
Talking it through with someone or asking questions
Trying it yourself and noticing what happens

questions.question: You notice a system at school, home, or work is confusing. What bothers you most?

options.text: People may struggle to use it or feel frustrated
It was not planned clearly from the beginning
It might fail, break, or slow people down
Important information may be handled carelessly

questions.question: Which activity sounds the most satisfying?

options.text: Turning a messy idea into something clear and structured
Spotting errors that others missed and fixing them
Creating something original that people enjoy seeing or using
Comparing details to discover patterns or useful answers

questions.question: If you had to choose one type of responsibility, which would you prefer?

options.text: Making sure people have a smooth and enjoyable experience
Making sure the structure behind everything is strong
Making sure problems are found before they get worse
Making sure ideas are imaginative and stand out

questions.question: What kind of thinking feels most natural to you?

options.text: How can this be simpler and clearer?
How can this be more reliable and better organized?
What is the hidden reason behind this result?
What could go wrong here that others may not notice?

questions.question: You are helping with an event. Which task would you choose first?

options.text: Welcoming people and making sure they feel comfortable
Creating the layout, flow, or visual style
Managing the setup so everything runs properly in the background
Checking details, timing, and anything that might fail

questions.question: Which compliment would feel the most meaningful to you?

options.text: You made this easy to understand and use.
You always come up with fresh and creative ideas.
You notice problems before anyone else does.
You are great at making complex things fit together.

questions.question: Which situation sounds most interesting to explore?

options.text: Why people choose one option over another
How a process can be made faster and smoother
How numbers, trends, or repeated behaviors tell a story
How a device, tool, or connected setup actually works

questions.question: If you were known for one strength, which would you want it to be?

options.text: Turning ideas into something people enjoy and connect with
Solving tricky problems patiently and carefully
Protecting important things and thinking ahead about risks
Understanding systems, patterns, and how everything connects

questions.question: The "Lego" Test: When building a set, do you follow the manual or build your own creation?

options.text: Follow the manual
Build my own creation

questions.question: The Problem Solver: Do you prefer fixing something broken or building something new?

options.text: Fixing something broken
Building something new

questions.question: The Visual Eye: Do you notice when a website's buttons are slightly off-center?

options.text: Yes, it bothers me
No, I rarely notice

questions.question: The Data Mind: Do you enjoy looking for patterns in spreadsheets or sports stats?

options.text: Yes, I love finding patterns
No, not my thing

questions.question: The Guardian: Does the idea of "locking down" a system and finding loopholes excite you?

options.text: Yes, absolutely
No, not really

questions.question: The Translator: Do you enjoy explaining complex ideas to people who don't get them?

options.text: Yes, I enjoy it
No, I prefer just doing the work

questions.question: The Architect: Do you prefer drawing the blueprint or actually swinging the hammer?

options.text: Drawing the blueprint
Swinging the hammer

questions.question: The Automation Bug: Do you get annoyed by repetitive tasks and try to find a shortcut?

options.text: Yes, I automate everything I can
No, I don't mind repetitive tasks

questions.question: The Device Preference: Are you more interested in how an app looks on a phone or how it works?

options.text: How it looks on a phone
How it works behind the scenes

questions.question: The Logic Puzzle: Do you enjoy math or logic games (Sudoku, Chess)?

options.text: Yes, I love them
No, I prefer other games

questions.question: The Speed Demon: Do you care more about how fast a game runs or how pretty it looks?

options.text: How fast it runs
How pretty it looks

questions.question: The Skeptic: When you see a "New Feature," is your first thought "How can I break this?"

options.text: Yes, exactly
No, I just want to use it

questions.question: The Organizer: Do you enjoy setting up the folders and "plumbing" of a project?

options.text: Yes, I like organizing the foundation
No, I want to jump straight into it

questions.question: The Storyteller: Do you like using data to prove a point or tell a story?

options.text: Yes, very much
No, data is just numbers to me

questions.question: The Deep Diver: Would you rather know a little about everything or be a master of one specific tool?

options.text: Know a little about everything
Master of one specific tool
